latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group designs and manufactures precision components for high‑performance industrial applications, mainly serving the automotive and aerospace markets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company supplies niche parts that are hard to source from larger manufacturers, giving it a defensible position in sectors that demand reliability and customization. Its products are critical to vehicle safety and aircraft performance, so demand is relatively inelastic.
company_30s.current_state: Allanfield is still in the early growth phase, operating on a modest cash pool and focusing on building a small but loyal customer base. The share price is trading well below the company’s net asset value, reflecting a steep discount that many investors see as a buying opportunity.

rns_narrative: The company has been steadily updating investors on its financial health, with the latest RNSs covering net asset value, AGM results, and a return‑of‑capital event that cleared a small tranche of shareholders. These announcements suggest the firm is maintaining liquidity and keeping shareholders informed, but they do not signal a major turnaround or new product launch.

There are two main themes emerging: 1) ongoing capital management – the firm is returning capital to shareholders while keeping enough cash for operations; 2) transparency – frequent updates keep the market informed, but no new catalysts have been announced yet. The company appears stable but still in a low‑activity phase.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet, indifferent chatter – the community is essentially sleeping on this stock.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No contemporary bulls are vocal; the few bullish remarks are dated from 2011 and refer to early IPO enthusiasm.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on the lack of recent catalysts and the company’s thin earnings track record. They warn that the firm’s cash burn could become unsustainable if revenue growth stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has remained flat for months; there has been no uptick in discussion or new viewpoints. The silence indicates that most market participants have moved on, leaving the stock in a contrarian position.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends data are flat, confirming that the stock is invisible to the broader market and that a surprise catalyst could trigger a significant reaction.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new customer contract or a partnership with a larger OEM would provide a clear revenue driver.
An unexpected capital raise or a debt‑free restructuring that improves cash flow could signal confidence from management.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds 3× the 30‑day average would indicate renewed interest and potential capitulation reversal.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the next quarterly earnings release for any sign of revenue growth or improved gross margins. Watch for a sudden jump in volume and a break above the 50‑day moving average, which would confirm a reversal of the deep crash.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle. It has already fallen sharply (≈86% from its peak), and the absence of volume suggests capitulation is complete. The APEX score indicates a tight compression zone, meaning a small price move could trigger a bounce if a catalyst appears.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price is at an extreme low, and the APEX score points to a tight trading range. If the stock can recover to halfway between its current trough and the pre‑crash level, that would be a 200% upside. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong: the market has priced out the company, and a single catalyst (new contract, capital raise, or volume spike) could unlock the hidden value.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s earnings history is thin and it has no recent catalysts. If revenue fails to improve or if the firm runs out of cash, the stock could stay stagnant or decline further. The worst case is a continued drift below the current support level, potentially leading to a prolonged flat or further decline.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the depth of the current trough (≈86% drop). Reward potential is a 2‑to‑1 upside if the stock recovers to the midpoint of the pre‑crash range. The asymmetry favors the trader: a small loss compared to a large potential gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 50‑day moving average on volume that is at least 2× the 30‑day average. Size the position to risk no more than 1% of capital per trade, and place a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear catalyst: a new customer order, a capital raise, or a significant volume spike. Only then consider a small position to test the bounce.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for insolvency, announces a delisting, or shows a drastic drop in cash reserves, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield Group sits at a steep 86% crash, is invisible to the market, and could rebound dramatically if a new contract or capital event surfaces, offering a 2‑to‑1 upside with limited downside.
